title: src/index.ts DecodeEvent and DecodedExtraChannel types diverge from dist/index.d.ts shipped contract
description: The shipped type declaration at dist/index.d.ts defines DecodedExtraChannel with a mandatory 'pixels: ArrayBuffer' and 'pixelFormat: PixelFormat' field (it extends ExtraChannelDescriptor with those added fields). src/index.ts defines DecodedExtraChannel as 'Readonly<Omit<ExtraChannel, ...>>' which has no pixels/pixelFormat at all. Additionally, dist declares 'extraChannelDescriptors?: readonly DecodedExtraChannel[]' on progress/final events, whereas src uses 'extraChannels?: readonly DecodedExtraChannel[]'. Consumers compiled against dist who read .extraChannelDescriptors[n].pixels will find the field missing at runtime if the src version is what actually ships. This is a structural mismatch between the authoring source and the emitted declaration.

title: src DecoderOptions is missing fields that dist/index.d.ts DecoderOptions omits — but both omit decodeExtraChannels/maxPixels/progressiveDetail that the runtime requires
description: The public DecoderOptions in src/index.ts declares decodeExtraChannels, maxPixels, and progressiveDetail as optional fields. The dist/index.d.ts DecoderOptions (the type callers see after install) has none of these fields. Tests in codec.test.ts pass decodeExtraChannels:true and maxPixels:1 at runtime, and these are forwarded to the native binding via the merged object at line 421. Consumers importing from the package will get a type error on these fields even though they are silently accepted at runtime. This is a schema omission in the published contract.

title: AnimationOptions.ticksPerSecond is required in src but optional in dist/index.d.ts
description: src/index.ts declares AnimationOptions as '{ ticksPerSecond: number; loopCount?: number; }' (ticksPerSecond required). dist/index.d.ts declares 'ticksPerSecond?: number' (optional). Callers using the dist types who omit ticksPerSecond will not get a compile error, but the runtime behaviour (default tps used vs explicit) may differ from intent. A test at codec.test.ts line 515 passes ticksPerSecond:100.5 and asserts exact round-trip to 4 decimal places — if the field is treated as optional and defaults are applied differently, the assertion may pass at 100 but fail on fractional values.

title: budget_exceeded DecodeEvent carries extraChannels in src but not in dist, creating consumer split
description: In src/index.ts the budget_exceeded event variant includes 'extraChannels?: readonly DecodedExtraChannel[]'. In dist/index.d.ts the budget_exceeded variant has no extraChannels field. Consumers compiled against dist cannot safely access extraChannels on budget_exceeded events even when present at runtime. The consumer that handles budget_exceeded and inspects extra-channel state (e.g. to composite a partial depth pass) will silently see undefined.

title: ExtraChannelType in src includes 'thermal', 'reserved0..7', and 'unknown' but dist ExtraChannel.type only allows 'alpha'|'depth'|'spot'|'selection'|'other'
description: src/index.ts defines ExtraChannelType as a wide union including 'thermal', 'reserved0'–'reserved7', and 'unknown'. The ExtraChannel interface in src/index.ts uses this wide type. dist/index.d.ts restricts ExtraChannel.type to 'alpha'|'depth'|'spot'|'selection'|'other'. Any caller who passes type:'thermal' or type:'reserved0' using the src types will get a type error when the dist types are used. This is a breaking schema difference for forward-compat channel types documented as intentional in src (Task 5 native parity comment).

title: guardEncoderOptions is a no-op — EncoderOptions has no input validation despite several invariants
description: guardEncoderOptions on line 373 is empty ('No capability guards remain'). EncoderOptions has several fields where invalid inputs can produce hard-to-debug native crashes or silent wrong output: (1) width/height of 0 passes validation and reaches native code; (2) distance and quality can both be non-null simultaneously with undefined precedence; (3) effort outside 1-9 is a TypeScript union constraint but arrives as any number from JS callers; (4) advancedFrameSettings can contain duplicate IDs after normalizeEncoderOptions merges them (the dedup only checks existing adv entries but the caller could supply duplicates for the same ID). A minimal guard for width>0, height>0, and mutual exclusion of distance vs quality would prevent native-layer errors.

title: NativeDecoder interface in src omits seekToFrame/seekToTime but wrapDecoder attaches them unconditionally
description: The NativeDecoder interface in src/index.ts (lines 240-246) declares only push/close/events/cancel/dispose. wrapDecoder (lines 377-411) unconditionally attaches seekToFrame and seekToTime to every wrapped decoder. dist/index.d.ts NativeDecoder does declare these as optional methods (lines 388-393). The src interface is therefore narrower than the runtime object, meaning code that TypeScript-checks against src/index.ts NativeDecoder will report seekToFrame/seekToTime as absent even though the runtime object has them. The facade.test.ts line 43 asserts 'typeof wrappedDecoder.seekToFrame === "function"' against the dist types; using the src type would not catch a regression here.

title: normalizeEncoderOptions silently merges advancedFrameSettings with no duplicate-ID guard
description: normalizeEncoderOptions builds the 'adv' array by appending entries from opts.advancedFrameSettings and then adding named-shortcut entries (progressiveDc, groupOrder, etc.) only if no existing entry has the same id. However, if the caller passes advancedFrameSettings with duplicate IDs (e.g. two entries with id:19), those duplicates pass through unchecked into the native binding. The native binding's behaviour with duplicate frame setting IDs is undefined — libjxl processes them in order and the last one wins silently. A dedup pass should compact caller-supplied duplicates before merging.

title: EncoderOptions in src lacks codestreamLevel, advancedControls, ecResampling, premultiply and many other fields present in dist — tests that exercise those fields cast around the missing types
description: facade.test.ts lines 62-106 exercise codestreamLevel, advancedControls (with buffering.streamingInput), and ecResampling. None of these fields are declared in src/index.ts EncoderOptions. The tests work around this with 'as typeof encodeOptions & { codestreamLevel: 10 }' casts. This means the src source of truth does not cover these fields, so any call site using src types will not be type-checked for them. The dist/index.d.ts is significantly more complete. The src file is the authoritative source for the package but it is clearly lagging the dist in type coverage.

title: No structural contract test verifies src/index.ts types match dist/index.d.ts at build time
description: The divergence between src/index.ts and dist/index.d.ts (findings contracts-1 through contracts-9) went undetected because there is no build-time check that the compiled dist matches the src. A simple 'tsd' or 'dtslint' test file that imports from both and asserts assignability in both directions would catch these shape drifts before they ship. Alternatively, generating dist from src as part of the CI pipeline (rather than maintaining them separately) would prevent drift entirely.
code_excerpt:
// No test file in packages/jxl-native/test/ imports from '../dist/index.d.ts'
// or cross-checks src and dist types for assignability.
